mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is directional when policy is turning down from a restrictive level and fails if rate cuts occur alongside rising inflation pressure.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines on a 3-month basis but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such episodes, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL acceleration above recent trend signals sticky inflation and raises the odds of tighter policy or higher real yields, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its 12-month moving average by at least 0.5pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation acceleration episodes are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal loses significance after the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE identifies weakening labor demand and rising recession odds. Once unemployment crosses a deterioration threshold, risk assets reprice lower and S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment rises are followed by positive SPX 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or if the 60-day Rank IC is persistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Strong RSAFS growth indicates resilient consumer spending and revenue momentum, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when sales growth is broad-based rather than a one-off spike.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth fails to produce above-median SPX 20-day returns over a 2-year rolling window, or if the direction flips negative in expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with rising PAYEMS signals an improving production-and-hiring cycle that lowers recession risk and supports equity earnings breadth. The combined signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days, and neither series alone should explain the effect as well as the pair.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-PAYEMS signal does not outperform each single-series model in walk-forward tests, or if positive 60-day SPX returns are not concentrated in episodes where both series are rising.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST marks housing-led demand weakness and tighter cyclical conditions, which typically precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is strongest when housing starts fall from an already elevated level.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by positive SPX 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the trigger adds no incremental predictive power over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while UNRATE falls, the economy is simultaneously expanding output and absorbing labor slack, which supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if output gains coincide with rising unemployment, implying low-quality or inventory-driven growth.
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO-up/UNRATE-down combination does not beat either proxy alone in out-of-sample Sharpe or if triggered periods do not show positive median SPX 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% combined with CPIAUCSL not falling indicates restrictive policy is not yet achieving disinflation, so equity valuations stay under pressure and S&P 500 returns turn negative over 5 trading days. The mechanism fails once inflation clearly decelerates or policy stops tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if the tightening-and-sticky-inflation condition is not followed by negative SPX 5-day returns in at least 60% of episodes, or if the effect disappears after inflation starts to decelerate.
